v.CNV: 这些国的人是耶和华留下的，为要借着他们来试验以色列人，就是所有没有经验过迦南任何战争的人；
v.ESV: Now these are the nations that the Lord left, to test Israel by them, that is, all in Israel who had not experienced all the wars in Canaan.
v.GAE: 여호와께서 가나안의 모든 전쟁들을 알지 못한 이스라엘을 시험하려 하시며
v.KJV: Now these are the nations which the LORD left, to prove Israel by them, even as many of Israel as had not known all the wars of Canaan;
v.NASB: Now these are the nations which the LORD left, to test Israel by them (that is, all who had not experienced any of the wars of Canaan;
v.NIV: These are the nations the Lord left to test all those Israelites who had not experienced any of the wars in Canaan
v.NRSV: Now these are the nations that the LORD left to test all those in Israel who had no experience of any war in Canaan
v.SAENEW: 가나안 전쟁을 전혀 겪어 본 일이 없는 모든 이스라엘 백성을 시험하시려고, 주님께서 그 땅에 남겨 두신 민족들이 있다.
v.WLB: 이들은 모든 가나안 전쟁을 한 번도 경험하지 못한 모든 이스라엘 자손들을 시험하기 위해 여호와께서 남겨 두신 민족들입니다.

v.CNV: 好使以色列人的后代，那些以前没有经验过这些战争的人，知道学习争战。
v.ESV: It was only in order that the generations of the people of Israel might know war, to teach war to those who had not known it before.
v.GAE: 이스라엘 자손의 세대 중에 아직 전쟁을 알지 못하는 자들에게 그것을 가르쳐 알게 하려 하사 남겨 두신 이방 민족들은
v.KJV: Only that the generations of the children of Israel might know, to teach them war, at the least such as before knew nothing thereof;
v.NASB: only in order that the generations of the sons of Israel might be taught war, those who had not experienced it formerly).
v.NIV: (he did this only to teach warfare to the descendants of the Israelites who had not had previous battle experience):
v.NRSV: (it was only that successive generations of Israelites might know war, to teach those who had no experience of it before):
v.SAENEW: 전에 전쟁을 겪어 본 일이 없는 이스라엘 자손의 세대들에게, 전쟁이 무엇인지 가르쳐 알게 하여 주려고 그들을 남겨 두신 것이다.
v.WLB: 이전에 전쟁을 경험하지 못한 이스라엘 자손들에게 전쟁을 가르치기 위한 것이었습니다.

v.CNV: 他留下的就是非利士人的五个首领，和所有迦南人、西顿人，以及住在黎巴嫩山上的希未人，从巴力．黑门山直到哈马口。
v.ESV: These are the nations: the five lords of the Philistines and all the Canaanites and the Sidonians and the Hivites who lived on Mount Lebanon, from Mount Baal-hermon as far as Lebo-hamath.
v.GAE: 블레셋의 다섯 군주들과 모든 가나안 족속과 시돈 족속과 바알 헤르몬 산에서부터 하맛 입구까지 레바논 산에 거주하는 히위 족속이라
v.KJV: Namely, five lords of the Philistines, and all the Canaanites, and the Sidonians, and the Hivites that dwelt in mount Lebanon, from mount Baal-hermon unto the entering in of Hamath.
v.NASB: These nations are: the five lords of the Philistines and all the Canaanites and the Sidonians and the Hivites who lived in Mount Lebanon, from Mount Baal-hermon as far as Lebo-hamath.
v.NIV: the five rulers of the Philistines, all the Canaanites, the Sidonians, and the Hivites living in the Lebanon mountains from Mount Baal Hermon to Lebo Hamath.
v.NRSV: the five lords of the Philistines, and all the Canaanites, and the Sidonians, and the Hivites who lived on Mount Lebanon, from Mount Baal-hermon as far as Lebo-hamath.
v.SAENEW: 그들은 바로 블레셋의 다섯 통치자와 가나안 사람 모두와 시돈 사람과 히위 사람이다. 히위 사람은 바알헤르몬 산으로부터 저 멀리 하맛 어귀에까지 이르는 레바논 산에 사는 사람들이다.
v.WLB: 이들은 블레셋의 다섯 군주들, 모든 가나안 사람들, 시돈 사람들 그리고 바알 헤르몬 산에서부터 하맛 입구까지의 레바논 산맥에 살고 있던 히위 사람들이었습니다.

v.CNV: 他们留在那里，为要借着他们试验以色列人，要知道他们是否听从耶和华借摩西吩咐他们列祖的诫命。
v.ESV: They were for the testing of Israel, to know whether Israel would obey the commandments of the Lord , which he commanded their fathers by the hand of Moses.
v.GAE: 남겨 두신 이 이방 민족들로 이스라엘을 시험하사 여호와께서 모세를 통하여 그들의 조상들에게 이르신 명령들을 순종하는지 알고자 하셨더라
v.KJV: And they were to prove Israel by them, to know whether they would hearken unto the commandments of the LORD, which he commanded their fathers by the hand of Moses.
v.NASB: They were for testing Israel, to find out if they would obey the commandments of the LORD, which He had commanded their fathers through Moses.
v.NIV: They were left to test the Israelites to see whether they would obey the Lord ’s commands, which he had given their ancestors through Moses.
v.NRSV: They were for the testing of Israel, to know whether Israel would obey the commandments of the LORD, which he commanded their ancestors by Moses.
v.SAENEW: 주님께서 이스라엘 자손을 시험하셔서, 그들이 과연 주님께서 모세를 시켜 조상들에게 내리신 명령에 순종하는지 순종하지 않는지를 알아보시려고 이런 민족들을 남겨놓으신 것이다.
v.WLB: 이들은 여호와께서 모세를 통해서 그들의 조상에게 명령하신 여호와의 계명에 귀를 기울이는지 이스라엘 자손들을 시험하기 위해서 남겨 두신 민족들이었습니다.

v.CNV: 以色列人就住在迦南人、赫人、亚摩利人、比利洗人、希未人、耶布斯人中间；
v.ESV: So the people of Israel lived among the Canaanites, the Hittites, the Amorites, the Perizzites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites.
v.GAE: 그러므로 이스라엘 자손은 가나안 족속과 헷 족속과 아모리 족속과 브리스 족속과 히위 족속과 여부스 족속 가운데에 거주하면서
v.KJV: And the children of Israel dwelt among the Canaanites, Hittites, and Amorites, and Perizzites, and Hivites, and Jebusites:
v.NASB: The sons of Israel lived among the Canaanites, the Hittites, the Amorites, the Perizzites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites;
v.NIV: The Israelites lived among the Canaanites, Hittites, Amorites, Perizzites, Hivites and Jebusites.
v.NRSV: So the Israelites lived among the Canaanites, the Hittites, the Amorites, the Perizzites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites;
v.SAENEW: 그래서 이스라엘 자손은 가나안 사람과 헷 사람과 아모리 사람과 브리스 사람과 히위 사람과 여부스 사람과 함께 섞여 살았다.
v.WLB: 이스라엘 자손들은 가나안 사람들과 헷 사람들과 아모리 사람들과 브리스 사람들과 히위 사람들과 여부스 사람들 가운데 살면서

v.CNV: 他们娶他们的女儿作妻子，也把自己的女儿嫁给他们的儿子，并且事奉他们的神。
v.ESV: And their daughters they took to themselves for wives, and their own daughters they gave to their sons, and they served their gods.
v.GAE: 그들의 딸들을 맞아 아내로 삼으며 자기 딸들을 그들의 아들들에게 주고 또 그들의 신들을 섬겼더라
v.KJV: And they took their daughters to be their wives, and gave their daughters to their sons, and served their gods.
v.NASB: and they took their daughters for themselves as wives, and gave their own daughters to their sons, and served their gods.
v.NIV: They took their daughters in marriage and gave their own daughters to their sons, and served their gods.
v.NRSV: and they took their daughters as wives for themselves, and their own daughters they gave to their sons; and they worshiped their gods.
v.SAENEW: 그리고 이스라엘 자손은 그 여러 민족의 딸을 데려다가 자기들의 아내로 삼았고, 또 자기들의 딸을 그들의 아들에게 주었으며, 그들의 신들을 섬겼다.
v.WLB: 그들의 딸들을 아내로 삼고 자기 딸들도 그들의 아들들에게 아내로 주면서 그들의 신들을 숭배했습니다.

v.CNV: 以色列人行了耶和华看为恶的事，忘记了耶和华他们的　神，去事奉众巴力和亚舍拉。
v.ESV: And the people of Israel did what was evil in the sight of the Lord . They forgot the Lord their God and served the Baals and the Asheroth.
v.GAE: 이스라엘 자손이 여호와의 목전에 악을 행하여 자기들의 하나님 여호와를 잊어버리고 바알들과 아세라들을 섬긴지라
v.KJV: And the children of Israel did evil in the sight of the LORD, and forgat the LORD their God, and served Baalim and the groves.
v.NASB: The sons of Israel did what was evil in the sight of the LORD, and forgot the LORD their God and served the Baals and the Asheroth.
v.NIV: The Israelites did evil in the eyes of the Lord ; they forgot the Lord their God and served the Baals and the Asherahs.
v.NRSV: The Israelites did what was evil in the sight of the LORD, forgetting the LORD their God, and worshiping the Baals and the Asherahs.
v.SAENEW: 이스라엘 자손이 주 하나님을 저버리고 바알과 아세라를 섬겨, 주님께서 보시기에 악한 일을 저질렀다.
v.WLB: 이스라엘 자손들은 여호와께서 보시기에 악한 일을 저질렀습니다. 그들은 그들의 하나님 여호와를 잊어버리고 바알과 아세라를 숭배했습니다.

v.CNV: 因此，耶和华的怒气向以色列人发作，把他们完全交在两河之间的亚兰王古珊．利萨田的手中；以色列人服事了古珊．利萨田八年。
v.ESV: Therefore the anger of the Lord was kindled against Israel, and he sold them into the hand of Cushan-rishathaim king of Mesopotamia. And the people of Israel served Cushan-rishathaim eight years.
v.GAE: 여호와께서 이스라엘에게 진노하사 그들을 메소보다미아 왕 구산 리사다임의 손에 파셨으므로 이스라엘 자손이 구산 리사다임을 팔 년 동안 섬겼더니
v.KJV: Therefore the anger of the LORD was hot against Israel, and he sold them into the hand of Chushan-rishathaim king of Mesopotamia: and the children of Israel served Chushan-rishathaim eight years.
v.NASB: Then the anger of the LORD was kindled against Israel, so that He sold them into the hands of Cushan-rishathaim king of Mesopotamia; and the sons of Israel served Cushan-rishathaim eight years.
v.NIV: The anger of the Lord burned against Israel so that he sold them into the hands of Cushan-Rishathaim king of Aram Naharaim, to whom the Israelites were subject for eight years.
v.NRSV: Therefore the anger of the LORD was kindled against Israel, and he sold them into the hand of King Cushan-rishathaim of Aram-naharaim; and the Israelites served Cushan-rishathaim eight years.
v.SAENEW: 주님께서 이스라엘 백성에게 크게 분노하시고, 그들을 메소포타미아 왕 구산리사다임의 손에 넘겨 주셨다. 이스라엘 자손이 구산리사다임을 여덟 해 동안 섬겼다.
v.WLB: 이스라엘에 대해 여호와의 분노가 불타올라 여호와께서는 그들을 메소포타미아 왕인 구산 리사다임의 손에 팔아 넘기셨습니다. 그리하여 이스라엘 자손들은 8년 동안 구산 리사다임을 섬겼습니다.

v.CNV: 以色列人向耶和华哀求的时候，耶和华就为以色列人兴起一位拯救者拯救他们，就是迦勒的弟弟基纳斯的儿子俄陀聂。
v.ESV: But when the people of Israel cried out to the Lord , the Lord raised up a deliverer for the people of Israel, who saved them, Othniel the son of Kenaz, Caleb's younger brother.
v.GAE: 이스라엘 자손이 여호와께 부르짖으매 여호와께서 이스라엘 자손을 위하여 한 구원자를 세워 그들을 구원하게 하시니 그는 곧 갈렙의 아우 그나스의 아들 옷니엘이라
v.KJV: And when the children of Israel cried unto the LORD, the LORD raised up a deliverer to the children of Israel, who delivered them, even Othniel the son of Kenaz, Caleb’s younger brother.
v.NASB: When the sons of Israel cried to the LORD, the LORD raised up a deliverer for the sons of Israel to deliver them, Othniel the son of Kenaz, Caleb's younger brother.
v.NIV: But when they cried out to the Lord , he raised up for them a deliverer, Othniel son of Kenaz, Caleb’s younger brother, who saved them.
v.NRSV: But when the Israelites cried out to the LORD, the LORD raised up a deliverer for the Israelites, who delivered them, Othniel son of Kenaz, Caleb’s younger brother.
v.SAENEW: 이스라엘 자손이 주님께 울부짖으니, 주님께서 그들을 구하여 주시려고 이스라엘 자손 가운데서 한 구원자를 세우셨는데, 그가 곧 갈렙의 아우 그나스의 아들인 옷니엘이다.
v.WLB: 그러나 이스라엘 자손들이 여호와께 부르짖을 때 여호와께서는 이스라엘 자손들을 위해 구원자를 일으켜 세우셨습니다. 여호와께서는 갈렙의 동생이자 그나스의 아들인 옷니엘을 통해서 그들을 구원하셨습니다.

v.CNV: 耶和华的灵临到他身上，他就治理以色列人；他出去作战，耶和华把亚兰王古珊．利萨田交在他手中，他的能力就胜过了古珊．利萨田。
v.ESV: The Spirit of the Lord was upon him, and he judged Israel. He went out to war, and the Lord gave Cushan-rishathaim king of Mesopotamia into his hand. And his hand prevailed over Cushan-rishathaim.
v.GAE: 여호와의 영이 그에게 임하셨으므로 그가 이스라엘의 사사가 되어 나가서 싸울 때에 여호와께서 메소보다미아 왕 구산 리사다임을 그의 손에 넘겨 주시매 옷니엘의 손이 구산 리사다임을 이기니라
v.KJV: And the Spirit of the LORD came upon him, and he judged Israel, and went out to war: and the LORD delivered Chushan-rishathaim king of Mesopotamia into his hand; and his hand prevailed against Chushan-rishathaim.
v.NASB: The Spirit of the LORD came upon him, and he judged Israel. When he went out to war, the LORD gave Cushan-rishathaim king of Mesopotamia into his hand, so that he prevailed over Cushan-rishathaim.
v.NIV: The Spirit of the Lord came on him, so that he became Israel’s judge and went to war. The Lord gave Cushan-Rishathaim king of Aram into the hands of Othniel, who overpowered him.
v.NRSV: The spirit of the LORD came upon him, and he judged Israel; he went out to war, and the LORD gave King Cushan-rishathaim of Aram into his hand; and his hand prevailed over Cushan-rishathaim.
v.SAENEW: 주님의 영이 그에게 내리니, 옷니엘은 이스라엘의 사사가 되어 전쟁터에 싸우러 나갔다. 주님께서 메소포타미아 왕 구산리사다임을 옷니엘의 손에 넘겨 주셨으므로, 옷니엘은 구산리사다임을 쳐서 이길 수 있었다.
v.WLB: 여호와의 성령이 옷니엘에게 임하셨고 그는 이스라엘의 사사가 됐습니다. 그가 전쟁에 나갔을 때 여호와께서 메소포타미아 왕 구산 리사다임을 그의 손에 넘겨주셔서 그가 구산 리사다임을 이길 수 있었습니다.

v.CNV: 于是国中太平了四十年。后来基纳斯的儿子俄陀聂死了。
v.ESV: So the land had rest for forty years. Then Othniel the son of Kenaz died.
v.GAE: 그 땅이 평온한 지 사십 년에 그나스의 아들 옷니엘이 죽었더라
v.KJV: And the land had rest forty years. And Othniel the son of Kenaz died.
v.NASB: Then the land had rest forty years. And Othniel the son of Kenaz died.
v.NIV: So the land had peace for forty years, until Othniel son of Kenaz died.
v.NRSV: So the land had rest forty years. Then Othniel son of Kenaz died.
v.SAENEW: 그 땅은 그나스의 아들 옷니엘이 죽을 때까지 사십 년 동안 전쟁이 없이 평온하였다.
v.WLB: 그러고 나서 그나스의 아들 옷니엘이 죽을 때까지 40년 동안 그 땅이 평온했습니다.

v.CNV: 以色列人又行了耶和华看为恶的事，耶和华就使摩押王伊矶伦强盛起来，欺压以色列人，因为他们行了耶和华看为恶的事。
v.ESV: And the people of Israel again did what was evil in the sight of the Lord , and the Lord strengthened Eglon the king of Moab against Israel, because they had done what was evil in the sight of the Lord .
v.GAE: 이스라엘 자손이 또 여호와의 목전에 악을 행하니라 이스라엘 자손이 여호와의 목전에 악을 행하므로 여호와께서 모압 왕 에글론을 강성하게 하사 그들을 대적하게 하시매
v.KJV: And the children of Israel did evil again in the sight of the LORD: and the LORD strengthened Eglon the king of Moab against Israel, because they had done evil in the sight of the LORD.
v.NASB: Now the sons of Israel again did evil in the sight of the LORD. So the LORD strengthened Eglon the king of Moab against Israel, because they had done evil in the sight of the LORD.
v.NIV: Again the Israelites did evil in the eyes of the Lord , and because they did this evil the Lord gave Eglon king of Moab power over Israel.
v.NRSV: The Israelites again did what was evil in the sight of the LORD; and the LORD strengthened King Eglon of Moab against Israel, because they had done what was evil in the sight of the LORD.
v.SAENEW: 이스라엘 자손이 다시 주님께서 보시기에 악한 일을 저질렀다. 그들이 이렇게 주님께서 보시기에 악한 일을 저질렀기 때문에, 주님께서는 모압 왕 에글론을 강적이 되게 하여서 이스라엘을 대적하게 하셨다.
v.WLB: 이스라엘 자손들이 여호와께서 보시기에 또다시 악한 일을 저질렀습니다. 여호와께서 보시기에 그들이 악한 일을 저질렀기 때문에 여호와께서는 모압 왕 에글론을 강하게 해 이스라엘에 맞서게 하셨습니다.

v.CNV: 伊矶伦集合了亚扪人和亚玛力人，前来击败了以色列人，占领了棕树城。
v.ESV: He gathered to himself the Ammonites and the Amalekites, and went and defeated Israel. And they took possession of the city of palms.
v.GAE: 에글론이 암몬과 아말렉 자손들을 모아 가지고 와서 이스라엘을 쳐서 종려나무 성읍을 점령한지라
v.KJV: And he gathered unto him the children of Ammon and Amalek, and went and smote Israel, and possessed the city of palm trees.
v.NASB: And he gathered to himself the sons of Ammon and Amalek; and he went and defeated Israel, and they possessed the city of the palm trees.
v.NIV: Getting the Ammonites and Amalekites to join him, Eglon came and attacked Israel, and they took possession of the City of Palms.
v.NRSV: In alliance with the Ammonites and the Amalekites, he went and defeated Israel; and they took possession of the city of palms.
v.SAENEW: 에글론은 암몬 자손과 아말렉 자손을 모아 이스라엘을 공격하고, 종려나무 성읍인 여리고를 점령하였다.
v.WLB: 에글론은 암몬과 아말렉 사람들을 모아서 이스라엘을 패배시키고 종려나무 성읍을 점령했습니다.

v.CNV: 于是以色列人服事了摩押王伊矶伦十八年。
v.ESV: And the people of Israel served Eglon the king of Moab eighteen years.
v.GAE: 이에 이스라엘 자손이 모압 왕 에글론을 열여덟 해 동안 섬기니라
v.KJV: So the children of Israel served Eglon the king of Moab eighteen years.
v.NASB: The sons of Israel served Eglon the king of Moab eighteen years.
v.NIV: The Israelites were subject to Eglon king of Moab for eighteen years.
v.NRSV: So the Israelites served King Eglon of Moab eighteen years.
v.SAENEW: 그래서 이스라엘 자손이 열여덟 해 동안이나 모압 왕 에글론을 섬겼다.
v.WLB: 그리하여 이스라엘 자손들은 18년 동안 모압 왕 에글론을 섬겼습니다.

v.CNV: 以色列人向耶和华哀求的时候，耶和华就为他们兴起一位拯救者，就是便雅悯人基拉的儿子以笏，是一个用左手的人。以色列人派他把贡物送给摩押王伊矶伦。
v.ESV: Then the people of Israel cried out to the Lord , and the Lord raised up for them a deliverer, Ehud, the son of Gera, the Benjaminite, a left-handed man. The people of Israel sent tribute by him to Eglon the king of Moab.
v.GAE: 이스라엘 자손이 여호와께 부르짖으매 여호와께서 그들을 위하여 한 구원자를 세우셨으니 그는 곧 베냐민 사람 게라의 아들 왼손잡이 에훗이라 이스라엘 자손이 그를 통하여 모압 왕 에글론에게 공물을 바칠 때에
v.KJV: But when the children of Israel cried unto the LORD, the LORD raised them up a deliverer, Ehud the son of Gera, a Benjamite, a man lefthanded: and by him the children of Israel sent a present unto Eglon the king of Moab.
v.NASB: But when the sons of Israel cried to the LORD, the LORD raised up a deliverer for them, Ehud the son of Gera, the Benjamite, a left-handed man. And the sons of Israel sent tribute by him to Eglon the king of Moab.
v.NIV: Again the Israelites cried out to the Lord , and he gave them a deliverer —Ehud , a left-handed man, the son of Gera the Benjamite. The Israelites sent him with tribute to Eglon king of Moab.
v.NRSV: But when the Israelites cried out to the LORD, the LORD raised up for them a deliverer, Ehud son of Gera, the Benjaminite, a left-handed man. The Israelites sent tribute by him to King Eglon of Moab.
v.SAENEW: 이스라엘 자손이 주님께 울부짖으니, 주님께서 그들에게 한 구원자를 세우셨는데, 그가 곧 베냐민 지파 게라의 아들인 왼손잡이 에훗이다. 이스라엘 자손은 에훗을 시켜, 모압 왕 에글론에게 조공을 보냈다.
v.WLB: 그러나 여호와께서는 이스라엘 자손들이 여호와께 부르짖을 때 베냐민 지파 게라의 아들인 왼손잡이 에훗을 그들의 구원자로 일으켜 세워 주셨습니다. 이스라엘 자손들은 모압 왕 에글론에게 바칠 선물을 그를 통해 보냈습니다.

v.CNV: 以笏做了一把两刃的剑，长半公尺，缚在右腿上，在衣服底下。
v.ESV: And Ehud made for himself a sword with two edges, a cubit in length, and he bound it on his right thigh under his clothes.
v.GAE: 에훗이 길이가 한 규빗 되는 좌우에 날선 칼을 만들어 그의 오른쪽 허벅지 옷 속에 차고
v.KJV: But Ehud made him a dagger which had two edges, of a cubit length; and he did gird it under his raiment upon his right thigh.
v.NASB: Ehud made himself a sword which had two edges, a cubit in length, and he bound it on his right thigh under his cloak.
v.NIV: Now Ehud had made a double-edged sword about a cubit long, which he strapped to his right thigh under his clothing.
v.NRSV: Ehud made for himself a sword with two edges, a cubit in length; and he fastened it on his right thigh under his clothes.
v.SAENEW: 그러자 에훗은 길이가 한 자쯤 되는 양쪽에 날이 선 칼을 만들어서 오른쪽 허벅지 옷 속에 차고,
v.WLB: 한편 에훗은 길이가 1규빗 정도 되는 양날을 가진 짧은 칼을 만들어 자기 옷 속 오른쪽 허벅지에 숨겼습니다.

v.CNV: 他把贡物呈献给摩押王伊矶伦；伊矶伦原是个非常肥胖的人。
v.ESV: And he presented the tribute to Eglon king of Moab. Now Eglon was a very fat man.
v.GAE: 공물을 모압 왕 에글론에게 바쳤는데 에글론은 매우 비둔한 자였더라
v.KJV: And he brought the present unto Eglon king of Moab: and Eglon was a very fat man.
v.NASB: He presented the tribute to Eglon king of Moab. Now Eglon was a very fat man.
v.NIV: He presented the tribute to Eglon king of Moab, who was a very fat man.
v.NRSV: Then he presented the tribute to King Eglon of Moab. Now Eglon was a very fat man.
v.SAENEW: 모압 왕 에글론에게 가서 조공을 바쳤다. 에글론은 살이 많이 찐 사람이었다.
v.WLB: 그러고는 모압 왕 에글론에게 선물을 가져갔습니다. 에글론은 매우 뚱뚱한 사람이었습니다.

v.CNV: 以笏献完了贡物，就把抬贡物的众人打发走了。
v.ESV: And when Ehud had finished presenting the tribute, he sent away the people who carried the tribute.
v.GAE: 에훗이 공물 바치기를 마친 후에 공물을 메고 온 자들을 보내고
v.KJV: And when he had made an end to offer the present, he sent away the people that bare the present.
v.NASB: It came about when he had finished presenting the tribute, that he sent away the people who had carried the tribute.
v.NIV: After Ehud had presented the tribute, he sent on their way those who had carried it.
v.NRSV: When Ehud had finished presenting the tribute, he sent the people who carried the tribute on their way.
v.SAENEW: 에훗은 조공을 바친 뒤에, 그 조공을 메고 온 사람들을 돌려보냈다.
v.WLB: 에훗은 선물을 바친 후에 그것을 운반한 사람들을 돌려보냈습니다.

v.CNV: 自己却从靠近吉甲的众雕像那里回来，说：“王啊，我有一件机密的事要对你说。”王说：“暂不要说。”侍立左右的人都离开他出去了。
v.ESV: But he himself turned back at the idols near Gilgal and said, “I have a secret message for you, O king.” And he commanded, “Silence.” And all his attendants went out from his presence.
v.GAE: 자기는 길갈 근처 돌 뜨는 곳에서부터 돌아와서 이르되 왕이여 내가 은밀한 일을 왕에게 아뢰려 하나이다 하니 왕이 명령하여 조용히 하라 하매 모셔 선 자들이 다 물러간지라
v.KJV: But he himself turned again from the quarries that were by Gilgal, and said, I have a secret errand unto thee, O king: who said, Keep silence. And all that stood by him went out from him.
v.NASB: But he himself turned back from the idols which were at Gilgal, and said, 'I have a secret message for you, O king.' And he said, 'Keep silence.' And all who attended him left him.
v.NIV: But on reaching the stone images near Gilgal he himself went back to Eglon and said, “Your Majesty, I have a secret message for you.” The king said to his attendants, “Leave us!” And they all left.
v.NRSV: But he himself turned back at the sculptured stones near Gilgal, and said, “I have a secret message for you, O king.” So the king said, “Silence!” and all his attendants went out from his presence.
v.SAENEW: 그러나 에훗 자신은 길갈 근처 돌 우상들이 있는 곳에서 되돌아와, 에글론에게 "임금님, 제가 은밀히 드릴 말씀이 있습니다" 하고 아뢰었다. 왕이, 모시고 섰던 부하들에게 물러가라고 명령하자, 그들은 모두 물러갔다.
v.WLB: 그러고는 길갈 근처에 있는 우상들이 있는 곳에서 다시 발길을 돌려 자기만 되돌아와서 말했습니다. “왕이여, 제가 왕께 은밀히 드릴 말씀이 있습니다.” 왕이 “조용히 하라!”라고 말하자 그의 모든 신하들이 밖으로 나갔습니다.

v.CNV: 以笏来到王那里；王独自一人坐在凉楼上。以笏说：“我有　神的话要告诉你。”王就从座位上站起来。
v.ESV: And Ehud came to him as he was sitting alone in his cool roof chamber. And Ehud said, “I have a message from God for you.” And he arose from his seat.
v.GAE: 에훗이 그에게로 들어가니 왕은 서늘한 다락방에 홀로 앉아 있는 중이라 에훗이 이르되 내가 하나님의 명령을 받들어 왕에게 아뢸 일이 있나이다 하매 왕이 그의 좌석에서 일어나니
v.KJV: And Ehud came unto him; and he was sitting in a summer parlour, which he had for himself alone. And Ehud said, I have a message from God unto thee. And he arose out of his seat.
v.NASB: Ehud came to him while he was sitting alone in his cool roof chamber. And Ehud said, 'I have a message from God for you.' And he arose from his seat.
v.NIV: Ehud then approached him while he was sitting alone in the upper room of his palace and said, “I have a message from God for you.” As the king rose from his seat,
v.NRSV: Ehud came to him, while he was sitting alone in his cool roof chamber, and said, “I have a message from God for you.” So he rose from his seat.
v.SAENEW: 에훗이 왕에게 다가섰을 때에, 마침 왕은 시원한 그의 집 다락방에 홀로 앉아 있었다. 에훗이 "임금님께 전하여 드릴 하나님의 말씀이 있습니다" 하니, 왕은 자리에서 일어섰다.
v.WLB: 그러자 에훗이 그에게 다가갔습니다. 왕은 지붕 위에 있는 시원한 다락방에 혼자 앉아 있었습니다. “제가 왕께 드릴 하나님의 말씀을 갖고 왔습니다”라고 에훗이 말했습니다. 그러자 왕이 자리에서 일어났습니다.

v.CNV: 以笏就伸出左手，从右腿上拔出剑来，刺入王的腹中；
v.ESV: And Ehud reached with his left hand, took the sword from his right thigh, and thrust it into his belly.
v.GAE: 에훗이 왼손을 뻗쳐 그의 오른쪽 허벅지 위에서 칼을 빼어 왕의 몸을 찌르매
v.KJV: And Ehud put forth his left hand, and took the dagger from his right thigh, and thrust it into his belly:
v.NASB: Ehud stretched out his left hand, took the sword from his right thigh and thrust it into his belly.
v.NIV: Ehud reached with his left hand, drew the sword from his right thigh and plunged it into the king’s belly.
v.NRSV: Then Ehud reached with his left hand, took the sword from his right thigh, and thrust it into Eglon’s belly;
v.SAENEW: 그 때에 에훗은 왼손으로 오른쪽 허벅지에서 칼을 뽑아 왕의 배를 찔렀다.
v.WLB: 그리고 에훗은 왼손을 뻗어 오른쪽 허벅지에서 칼을 뽑아 왕의 배를 찔렀습니다.

v.CNV: 连剑柄与剑身都刺进去了，肥肉把剑身夹住，因此他没有把剑从王的肚腹里拔出来；接着他就从窗户爬了出去。
v.ESV: And the hilt also went in after the blade, and the fat closed over the blade, for he did not pull the sword out of his belly; and the dung came out.
v.GAE: 칼자루도 날을 따라 들어가서 그 끝이 등 뒤까지 나갔고 그가 칼을 그의 몸에서 빼내지 아니하였으므로 기름이 칼날에 엉겼더라
v.KJV: And the haft also went in after the blade; and the fat closed upon the blade, so that he could not draw the dagger out of his belly; and the dirt came out.
v.NASB: The handle also went in after the blade, and the fat closed over the blade, for he did not draw the sword out of his belly; and the refuse came out.
v.NIV: Even the handle sank in after the blade, and his bowels discharged. Ehud did not pull the sword out, and the fat closed in over it.
v.NRSV: the hilt also went in after the blade, and the fat closed over the blade, for he did not draw the sword out of his belly; and the dirt came out.
v.SAENEW: 칼자루까지도 칼날을 따라 들어가서 칼 끝이 등 뒤로 나왔다. 에훗이 그 칼을 빼내지 않았으므로, 기름기가 칼에 엉겨 붙었다.
v.WLB: 칼의 손잡이도 칼날을 따라 들어갔습니다. 에훗이 칼을 왕의 배에서 빼내지 않아 몸속의 지방이 칼날을 감싸고 등 뒤로 나왔습니다.

v.CNV: 以笏出到走廊，把凉楼上的门关起来，上了锁。
v.ESV: Then Ehud went out into the porch and closed the doors of the roof chamber behind him and locked them.
v.GAE: 에훗이 현관에 나와서 다락문들을 뒤에서 닫아 잠그니라
v.KJV: Then Ehud went forth through the porch, and shut the doors of the parlour upon him, and locked them.
v.NASB: Then Ehud went out into the vestibule and shut the doors of the roof chamber behind him, and locked them.
v.NIV: Then Ehud went out to the porch ; he shut the doors of the upper room behind him and locked them.
v.NRSV: Then Ehud went out into the vestibule, and closed the doors of the roof chamber on him, and locked them.
v.SAENEW: 에훗은 현관으로 나가, 뒤에서 다락방 문을 닫고 걸어 잠갔다.
v.WLB: 에훗은 곧 밖으로 나와서 다락방의 문들을 닫고서 걸어 잠갔습니다.

v.CNV: 以笏出来以后，王的仆人才来到；他们看见凉楼上的门锁着，就说：“王一定是在凉楼上大解。”
v.ESV: When he had gone, the servants came, and when they saw that the doors of the roof chamber were locked, they thought, “Surely he is relieving himself in the closet of the cool chamber.”
v.GAE: 에훗이 나간 후에 왕의 신하들이 들어와서 다락문들이 잠겼음을 보고 이르되 왕이 분명히 서늘한 방에서 그의 발을 가리우신다 하고
v.KJV: When he was gone out, his servants came; and when they saw that, behold, the doors of the parlour were locked, they said, Surely he covereth his feet in his summer chamber.
v.NASB: When he had gone out, his servants came and looked, and behold, the doors of the roof chamber were locked; and they said, 'He is only relieving himself in the cool room.'
v.NIV: After he had gone, the servants came and found the doors of the upper room locked. They said, “He must be relieving himself in the inner room of the palace.”
v.NRSV: After he had gone, the servants came. When they saw that the doors of the roof chamber were locked, they thought, “He must be relieving himself in the cool chamber.”
v.SAENEW: 에훗이 나간 뒤에, 그의 부하들이 와서 다락방 문이 잠겨 있는 것을 보고, 왕이 그 시원한 다락방에서 용변을 보고 있다고 생각하였다.
v.WLB: 그가 가고 난 후에 신하들이 와서 다락방의 문들이 잠겨 있는 것을 보고서 “왕께서 시원한 다락방에서 용변을 보고 계시나 보다”라고 말했습니다.

v.CNV: 他们等到发慌了，见他还不打开凉楼的门，就拿钥匙来开；不料，看见他们的主人早已倒在地上死了。
v.ESV: And they waited till they were embarrassed. But when he still did not open the doors of the roof chamber, they took the key and opened them, and there lay their lord dead on the floor.
v.GAE: 그들이 오래 기다려도 왕이 다락문들을 열지 아니하는지라 열쇠를 가지고 열어 본즉 그들의 군주가 이미 땅에 엎드러져 죽었더라
v.KJV: And they tarried till they were ashamed: and, behold, he opened not the doors of the parlour; therefore they took a key, and opened them: and, behold, their lord was fallen down dead on the earth.
v.NASB: They waited until they became anxious; but behold, he did not open the doors of the roof chamber. Therefore they took the key and opened them, and behold, their master had fallen to the floor dead.
v.NIV: They waited to the point of embarrassment, but when he did not open the doors of the room, they took a key and unlocked them. There they saw their lord fallen to the floor, dead.
v.NRSV: So they waited until they were embarrassed. When he still did not open the doors of the roof chamber, they took the key and opened them. There was their lord lying dead on the floor.
v.SAENEW: 그러나 오랫동안 기다려도 왕이 끝내 다락방 문을 열지 않으므로, 열쇠를 가져다가 문을 열고 보니, 왕이 죽은 채로 바닥에 쓰러져 있었다.
v.WLB: 그들은 오랫동안 기다렸습니다. 왕이 여전히 다락방 문을 열지 않아 그들이 열쇠를 가져다가 열어 보니 그들의 왕이 바닥에 쓰러져 죽어 있었습니다.

v.CNV: 他们耽延的时候，以笏已经逃跑了；他经过众雕像那里，逃到西伊拉去。
v.ESV: Ehud escaped while they delayed, and he passed beyond the idols and escaped to Seirah.
v.GAE: 그들이 기다리는 동안에 에훗이 피하여 돌 뜨는 곳을 지나 스이라로 도망하니라
v.KJV: And Ehud escaped while they tarried, and passed beyond the quarries, and escaped unto Seirath.
v.NASB: Now Ehud escaped while they were delaying, and he passed by the idols and escaped to Seirah.
v.NIV: While they waited, Ehud got away. He passed by the stone images and escaped to Seirah.
v.NRSV: Ehud escaped while they delayed, and passed beyond the sculptured stones, and escaped to Seirah.
v.SAENEW: 그들이 기다리는 동안에 에훗은 몸을 피하여, 돌 우상들이 있는 곳을 지나서 스이라로 도망쳤다.
v.WLB: 한편 그들이 기다리고 있는 동안 에훗은 도망쳤습니다. 그는 우상들이 있는 곳을 지나 스이라로 도망쳤습니다.

v.CNV: 他去到以后，就在以法莲山地吹角；以色列人与他一同从山地上下来，他走在他们前头，
v.ESV: When he arrived, he sounded the trumpet in the hill country of Ephraim. Then the people of Israel went down with him from the hill country, and he was their leader.
v.GAE: 그가 이르러 에브라임 산지에서 나팔을 불매 이스라엘 자손이 산지에서 그를 따라 내려오니 에훗이 앞서 가며
v.KJV: And it came to pass, when he was come, that he blew a trumpet in the mountain of Ephraim, and the children of Israel went down with him from the mount, and he before them.
v.NASB: It came about when he had arrived, that he blew the trumpet in the hill country of Ephraim; and the sons of Israel went down with him from the hill country, and he was in front of them.
v.NIV: When he arrived there, he blew a trumpet in the hill country of Ephraim, and the Israelites went down with him from the hills, with him leading them.
v.NRSV: When he arrived, he sounded the trumpet in the hill country of Ephraim; and the Israelites went down with him from the hill country, having him at their head.
v.SAENEW: 그가 그 곳에 이르러 에브라임 산간지방에서 소집 나팔을 불자, 이스라엘 자손이 그를 따라 산간지방에서 쳐내려갔다. 에훗이 그들을 앞장섰다.
v.WLB: 그가 그곳에 도착해 에브라임 산지에서 나팔을 불자 이스라엘 자손들이 그를 앞장세우고 그와 함께 산에서 내려왔습니다.

v.CNV: 对他们说：“你们跟随我，因为耶和华已经把你们的仇敌摩押人交在你们手中。”他们就跟随他下去，攻取约旦河的渡口，拦截摩押人，不让一个过去。
v.ESV: And he said to them, “Follow after me, for the Lord has given your enemies the Moabites into your hand.” So they went down after him and seized the fords of the Jordan against the Moabites and did not allow anyone to pass over.
v.GAE: 그들에게 이르되 나를 따르라 여호와께서 너희의 원수들인 모압을 너희의 손에 넘겨 주셨느니라 하매 무리가 에훗을 따라 내려가 모압 맞은편 요단 강 나루를 장악하여 한 사람도 건너지 못하게 하였고
v.KJV: And he said unto them, Follow after me: for the LORD hath delivered your enemies the Moabites into your hand. And they went down after him, and took the fords of Jordan toward Moab, and suffered not a man to pass over.
v.NASB: He said to them, 'Pursue them, for the LORD has given your enemies the Moabites into your hands.' So they went down after him and seized the fords of the Jordan opposite Moab, and did not allow anyone to cross.
v.NIV: “Follow me,” he ordered, “for the Lord has given Moab, your enemy, into your hands. ” So they followed him down and took possession of the fords of the Jordan that led to Moab; they allowed no one to cross over.
v.NRSV: He said to them, “Follow after me; for the LORD has given your enemies the Moabites into your hand.” So they went down after him, and seized the fords of the Jordan against the Moabites, and allowed no one to cross over.
v.SAENEW: "나를 따라라! 주님께서 너희 원수 모압을 너희 손에 넘겨 주셨다." 그가 이렇게 외치자, 그들이 에훗을 따라 내려가 모압으로 가는 요단 강 나루를 점령하고 한 사람도 건너가지 못하게 하였다.
v.WLB: 에훗이 말했습니다. “나를 따르라. 여호와께서 너희 원수인 모압 사람들을 너희 손에 주셨다.” 그래서 그들은 그를 따라 내려가서 모압으로 가는 요단 강 나루터를 차지하고 아무도 건너지 못하게 했습니다.

v.CNV: 那时他们击杀了约有一万摩押人，都是强壮的、勇猛的人，没有一人逃脱。
v.ESV: And they killed at that time about 10,000 of the Moabites, all strong, able-bodied men; not a man escaped.
v.GAE: 그 때에 모압 사람 약 만 명을 죽였으니 모두 장사요 모두 용사라 한 사람도 도망하지 못하였더라
v.KJV: And they slew of Moab at that time about ten thousand men, all lusty, and all men of valour; and there escaped not a man.
v.NASB: They struck down at that time about ten thousand Moabites, all robust and valiant men; and no one escaped.
v.NIV: At that time they struck down about ten thousand Moabites, all vigorous and strong; not one escaped.
v.NRSV: At that time they killed about ten thousand of the Moabites, all strong, able-bodied men; no one escaped.
v.SAENEW: 그 때에 그들이 쳐죽인 건장하고 용맹스러운 모압 군인의 수는 모두 만 명이나 되었는데, 한 사람도 도망치지 못하였다.
v.WLB: 그때 그들이 모압 사람 1만 명 정도를 죽였는데 그들은 모두 건장하고 용맹한 사람들이었으며 단 한 사람도 살아남지 못했습니다.

v.CNV: 这样，从那天起，摩押就在以色列人的手下被制伏了，于是国中太平了八十年。
v.ESV: So Moab was subdued that day under the hand of Israel. And the land had rest for eighty years.
v.GAE: 그 날에 모압이 이스라엘 수하에 굴복하매 그 땅이 팔십 년 동안 평온하였더라
v.KJV: So Moab was subdued that day under the hand of Israel. And the land had rest fourscore years.
v.NASB: So Moab was subdued that day under the hand of Israel. And the land was undisturbed for eighty years.
v.NIV: That day Moab was made subject to Israel, and the land had peace for eighty years.
v.NRSV: So Moab was subdued that day under the hand of Israel. And the land had rest eighty years.
v.SAENEW: 그 날 모압은 굴복하여 이스라엘의 통치를 받게 되었고, 그 뒤로 그 땅에는 팔십 년 동안 전쟁이 없이 평온하였다.
v.WLB: 그날 모압은 이스라엘의 손에 굴복했고 그 땅이 80년 동안 평온했습니다.

v.CNV: 以笏之后，有亚拿的儿子珊迦；他用赶牛棒击杀了六百非利士人；他也拯救了以色列人。
v.ESV: After him was Shamgar the son of Anath, who killed 600 of the Philistines with an oxgoad, and he also saved Israel.
v.GAE: 에훗 후에는 아낫의 아들 삼갈이 있어 소 모는 막대기로 블레셋 사람 육백 명을 죽였고 그도 이스라엘을 구원하였더라
v.KJV: And after him was Shamgar the son of Anath, which slew of the Philistines six hundred men with an ox goad: and he also delivered Israel.
v.NASB: After him came Shamgar the son of Anath, who struck down six hundred Philistines with an oxgoad; and he also saved Israel.
v.NIV: After Ehud came Shamgar son of Anath, who struck down six hundred Philistines with an oxgoad. He too saved Israel.
v.NRSV: After him came Shamgar son of Anath, who killed six hundred of the Philistines with an oxgoad. He too delivered Israel.
v.SAENEW: 에훗 다음에는 아낫의 아들 삼갈이 사사가 되었다. 그는 소를 모는 막대기만으로 블레셋 사람 육백 명을 쳐죽여 이스라엘을 구하였다.
v.WLB: 아낫의 아들 삼갈이 에훗의 뒤를 이었는데 그는 소를 모는 막대기로 블레셋 사람 600명을 죽이고 그 또한 이스라엘을 구원했습니다.
